results.rendered_html: mahā—kāvya n. a great or classical poem (applied as a distinguishing title to 6 chief artificial poems, viz. the Raghu-vaṃśa, Kumārasambhava and Megha-dūta by Kālidāsa, the Śiśupāla-vadha by Māgha, the Kirātārjunīya by Bhāravi and the Naiṣadha-carita by Śrī-harṣa; accord. to some the Bhaṭṭi-kāvya is also a M˚),  Kāvyād. ;  Pratāp.  ( IW. 452 ).

results.rendered_html: mahākāvya (ma˚ + 2. kā˚) n. ein grosses —, ein klassisches Dichtwerk  TRIK. 3,2,22 .  KĀVYĀD. 1,14 .  PRATĀPAR. 19,a,3 .  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 211,a,44 . Dieses ehrenvolle Prädicat erhalten folgende sechs Dichtungen: Raghuvaṃśa, Kumārasaṃbhava, Meghadūta, Śiśupālavadha, Kirātārjunīya und Naiṣadhacarita,  COLEBR. Misc. Ess. 2,84 . Nach dem  ŚKDR.  gehört auch das Bhaṭṭikāvya hierher.
